algorithms.title: Tracheostomy Emergency Troubleshooting
algorithms.description: Field sequence for a child with a tracheostomy in respiratory distress.
steps: Recognize respiratory distress in a child with a tracheostomy and engage the caregiver immediately for baseline and equipment information.
Attempt suctioning through the tracheostomy tube to clear a possible mucus plug.
If suctioning does not resolve distress, check for accidental decannulation (tube fully or partially displaced).
If decannulated, replace with a same-size tracheostomy tube (or appropriately sized endotracheal tube) if trained and equipped.
If the tube cannot be quickly replaced, provide supplemental oxygen/ventilation via the tracheostomy stoma.
Reassess oxygenation and work of breathing continuously; transport rapidly.

algorithms.title: CSHCN Field Assessment Using Caregiver-Reported Baseline
algorithms.description: Approach for interpreting findings in a child with special healthcare needs by anchoring to personal baseline.
steps: Ask the caregiver about the child's usual baseline vital signs, oxygen saturation, mental status, and typical emergency presentation.
Compare current findings to the reported baseline rather than typical population norms.
Identify any acute change from that baseline as the primary marker of a new problem.
Use the caregiver as an active partner in troubleshooting equipment (tracheostomy, ventilator, shunt, feeding tube, central line) throughout the call.
Communicate the child's baseline and any acute changes clearly during receiving-facility handoff.
